postmortem.primary_read: NYK won Game 1 despite SAS having slightly better shot quality and higher rim-or-three frequency. The deciding edge was conversion, turnover control, and defensive rebounding stability. SAS produced a playable shot map but converted poorly from three and lost the turnover battle.
postmortem.scoring_read: NYK and SAS both played at 99 possessions. NYK scored 105 with 50.97% TS and 46.84% eFG. SAS scored 95 with 47.03% TS and 42.13% eFG. SAS had 0.54 shot quality versus NYK 0.53 but failed to convert.
postmortem.shot_profile_read: SAS generated 74.16% rim-or-three frequency but made only 25.58% from three. NYK generated 69.47% rim-or-three frequency and shot 29.73% from three. Neither team shot well, but NYK was less inefficient and better inside the arc.
postmortem.rebounding_read: NYK won total rebounds 59-57 while offensive rebounds were even at 16-16. Josh Hart was the key swing profile with 15 rebounds on 17 chances and 88.2% adjusted chance rate. Towns had huge opportunity volume with 12 rebounds on 25 chances, while Robinson added 6 rebounds in only 13.2 minutes with 3 box outs.
postmortem.sas_rebounding_read: SAS had strong wing rebound conversion: Vassell 9 boards on 10 chances, Harper 8 on 11 chances, Champagnie 10 on 14 chances, Castle 8 on 11 chances. Wembanyama had 12 boards on 22 chances, but NYK still kept the glass neutral enough to win.
postmortem.turnover_read: NYK had 9 turnovers and SAS had 13. SAS also had 8 live-ball turnovers compared to NYK's 4. This was the biggest non-shooting separator because it wiped away SAS' shot-quality advantage.
postmortem.foul_read: Both teams committed 23 fouls and drew 23 fouls. The whistle was balanced and did not create a team-specific distortion. This supports treating Game 1 as a clean efficiency/turnover outcome rather than a referee-driven result.

game2_prep_flags.description: SAS had better shot quality and higher rim-or-three rate but lost due to poor 3P conversion and turnovers. Watch for market overcorrection against SAS.
Josh Hart converted 15 rebounds on 17 chances. Maintain elevated rebound baseline if minutes remain near 27+ and SAS continues long-miss volume.
KAT had 25 rebound chances but only 48% raw chance conversion. Volume supports rebounds, but conversion risk remains.
SAS had 8 live-ball turnovers and 13 total turnovers. Downgrade SAS spread/ML if early Game 2 live-ball turnovers persist.
Game landed 200 on 99 possessions each, showing efficiency drag more than pace drag. Total market should be judged by conversion assumptions, not pace alone.

baseline_prior_before_market.notes: This is a pre-market stub from Game 1 diagnostics only. Needs current Game 2 lines, venue, injuries, and expected minutes before actionable edge classification.
